Station Summary Data

Amanda Liczner

Overview of the data from Motus Project #1 summary data (downloaded off the web).

Data preparation summary

The summary data was downloaded from all Motus Project #1 towers. The data are downloaded as “pairs” with the current and previous station noted

A preview downloaded data:

Additional columns were added:

  • column for season
    • spring = April 15 - June 15
    • summer = June 15 - August 18
    • fall = August 15 - November 15
    • Winter = November 15- April 15
## 
##   Fall Migration Spring Migration           Summer           Winter 
##            50692            30960            37672            22979
  • column for sunrise/sunset time
  • column for sunrise/sunset local time
  • column for movement duration in hours

Data cleaning

columns added continued:

  • a column for whether the detection is likely a flight (< 12 hrs duration) or an incident (> 12 hr duration)
## 
##    flight incidence 
##     20258     15607

cleaning detections that went back in time

There are detections where the end time is before the start time. These are some sort of error and need to be removed

The number of detections after removing the backwards detections:

## [1] 27309

cleaning detections that are false positives and local overlaps

Two other data problems exist:

  • false positives
  • local overlapping stations picking up the same individual

local overlap was distinguished from false positives by comparing speed and distance. Local overalp would have:

  • a distance of <= 30 km between stations
  • a speed > 120 km/h

…and false positives:

  • any remaining detections with speeds > 120 km/h

False positives were removed

Local overlap detections were corrected by calculating the centroid between the two stations, replacing the coordinates with this new centroid location, recalculating the distance, replacing the name of the current station as a combination to the two stations, and for the next detection in sequence, replacing the previous station information with the new centroid information.

## 
##         FALSE_POSITIVE          LOCAL_OVERLAP LOCAL_OVERLAP_RESOLVED 
##                    389                     17                    120 
##           VALID_FLIGHT 
##                  26655

Number of detections after removing false positives and resolving local overlap

## [1] 27181

Flight information added

Flight specific information was added as additional columns This includes:

  • flight number (sequential flights per individual)
  • speed per step in the flight
  • turning angle (where it could be calculated)
  • diel period (daylight or night) of the flight
  • hours from sunrise and sunset (can be used to identify flights close to take-off or landing)
  • additional grouping variable added:
    • which subbasin the occurrence took place in within the GLWS

Individual information added

Information about each individual was added including:

  • the tag deployment coordinates
  • sex/age of the individual (if the project included this)
  • days post migration the detection occurred
  • tagging location potential issue flags
    • whether the tagging location was inside or outside the GLWS
    • how far from the boundary and the closest lake shoreline the tagging location was

Data Flags

There are two columns of data flags

  1. Valid Distance:
  • for station pairs that are < 30 km apart = FALSE_LESS
  • for station pairs that are > 965 km apart = FALSE_MORE

Pairs that are close together are likely local overlaps Pairs that are too far apart are likely false positives

## 
## FALSE_LESS FALSE_MORE       TRUE 
##      12538       1782      12861
  1. Tagging location flags for spring phenology
  • if the tagging location is within the GLWS = Flag_1
  • if the tagging location is within the GLWS and far inland >5 km = Flag_2
  • if the tagging location is within the GLWS and not during migration (was tagged in summer or winter) = Flag_3
  • if the tagging location information is missing = Flag_4

These are flagged to be investigated as potential issues for assessing spring arrival phenology

## 
## Flag 1 Flag 2 Flag 3 Flag 4   None 
##   6374   7533   3253   3794   6227

Spring phenology

filter by spring season and remove problematic tagging site flags

Keep only observations that:

  • occurred in spring
  • had no tagging site flags
  • or were Flag_1 (within the GLWS but <5 km from the shoreline)
